lines.text: Cardboard boxes. A single mattress. The smell of fresh paint and someone else's cooking from down the hall. You made it — KL, first job, first apartment.
It's smaller than you imagined. But it's yours. Well — half yours.

lines.speaker: Aiden
lines.text: Weh, finally! I was starting to think you got lost. I already took the bigger room — hope you don't mind. Also the wifi password is 'ilovemoney123'. I thought it was motivational.
He grins like he's known you for years. You've spoken maybe three times in a Facebook group. Your phone buzzes.
📧 HR Notification: Welcome aboard! Your first salary of RM 2,500 will be credited on the 25th. Please complete your onboarding by Friday.
RM 2,500. The number sits in your chest like something heavy and exciting at the same time.

choices.text: Finally. First thing — I'm making a proper savings plan.
I already know what I'm spending it on. I earned this.
Honestly? I have no idea what I'm doing.

lines.text: You're making coffee when someone walks in. Pink hair, sharp eyes, a thermos in one hand.

lines.speaker: Ms. Clara
lines.text: You must be the new one. Clara Lim — finance team, two floors up. First day?

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: Yeah. Still figuring out where the bathroom is honestly.

lines.speaker: Ms. Clara
lines.text: Fair. That took me a week. Hey — do you have a plan for your salary yet? I'm not being weird. I just wish someone had asked me that when I started. I spent mine in nine days.

choices.text: Sort of. I want to save but not sure how much.
Not really. I'll see how it goes.
I have no idea. Should I?

lines.speaker: Ms. Clara
lines.text: Okay so — 50% needs, 30% wants, 20% savings. You don't have to be perfect about it. Just start somewhere.

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: That's actually... simple enough.

lines.speaker: Ms. Clara
lines.text: Right? Nobody teaches us this. Anyway — bathroom is down the left corridor, past the always-broken printer.
She leaves. You didn't expect the first useful thing you'd learn at this job to happen before you even sat down.

lines.speaker: Ms. Clara
lines.text: Fair enough. First week is always a blur anyway. I'm Clara — if you ever want to talk money stuff, you know where to find me.
Aiden is home before you. Shoes off, three Shopee tabs open, very comfortable in his slightly-larger room.

lines.speaker: Aiden
lines.text: Bro! First day done. How was it? Eh — there's a flash sale tonight. Mechanical keyboard, was RM 400, now RM 180. You want to split shipping?

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: I haven't even gotten paid yet.

lines.speaker: Aiden
lines.text: So? Only RM 180. Investment in productivity. Aiden-certified, 100% recommended.

choices.text: I'll pass. Not spending before I see my account.

lines.text: Your phone lights up. It's Evelyn.

lines.speaker: Evelyn
lines.text: OKAY I need to tell someone — I just signed my offer letter. I start next week. Are you panicking? Because I'm panicking a little.

lines.speaker: You
lines.text: A little. My roommate is already trying to get me to spend money.

lines.speaker: Evelyn
lines.text: Oh no. Is he the fun kind of bad influence or the actually dangerous kind?

lines.speaker: Evelyn
lines.text: Can we just figure this out together? Tell each other what we're doing with our salaries and keep each other honest?

choices.text: Sure — I also met someone at work who might actually know what she's doing.

lines.text: You can't sleep. You open your banking app. Refresh. Then — RM 2,500. You stare at it longer than you mean to.
Three notifications arrive almost at once.
Ms. Clara: Salary day. Do you have a plan yet? — C.L.
Aiden: BROOO ITS IN 🔥 mamak tonight my treat (you pay me back later lol)
Evelyn: okay I got mine too. I have absolutely no idea what to do. please help.
RM 2,500. One month. Three voices pulling in different directions. What's your move?

choices.text: Reply to Ms. Clara — I'm ready to make a plan.
Reply to Aiden — mamak sounds good.
Reply to Evelyn — let's figure it out together.

lines.text: Morning comes fast. RM 2,500 is sitting in your account. Rent is due in two weeks. Time to figure out where it all goes.

lines.speaker: Ms. Clara
lines.text: Okay. Open your notes app. Let's map this out before you spend a single ringgit.
